title: rosa gymnocarpa subsp. helleri
intro: Rosa gymnocarpa subsp. helleri, also known as Heller's rose or mountain rose, is a subspecies of shrub that is native to the western United States and is a member of the rose family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Rosa gymnocarpa subsp. helleri include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to aid in the healing of wounds and skin irritations. Rosa gymnocarpa subsp. helleri is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, salves, and infusions, which can be made by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water.
